topic.description: This cluster focuses on various species of marine animals, particularly sharks and other aquatic organisms, highlighting their characteristics, habitats, and behaviors.

7: The copper shark is distributed in a number of separate populations in the northeastern and southwestern Atlantic.
It is characterized by its narrow, hook-shaped upper teeth.
8: The whitetip reef shark is one of the most common sharks found on Indo-Pacific coral reefs.
It is typically found on or near the bottom in clear water.
9: The dusky shark is a generalist apex predator occurring in tropical and warm-temperate continental seas worldwide.
It has a broad and varied diet, consisting mostly of bony fishes, sharks and rays.
10: Sharks are a group of fish characterized by a cartilaginous skeleton.
Modern sharks are classified within the clade Selachimorpha.

topic.name: Physics and Material Science
topic.description: The documents discuss various concepts in physics and material science, including electronic circuit topology, properties of alkali metals, genetic drift in biology, crystallographic defects in diamond, condensed matter physics, and liquid crystals.

1: The topology of an electronic circuit is the form taken by the network of interconnections of the circuit components.
Topology is not concerned with the physical layout of components in a circuit.
2: The alkali metals are a group in the periodic table consisting of the chemical elements lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, caesium, and francium.
They are all shiny, soft, highly reactive metals at standard temperature and pressure.
3: Genetic drift is the change in the frequency of a gene variant in a population due to random sampling of organisms.
Genetic drift may cause gene variants to disappear completely and thereby reduce genetic variation.
4: Imperfections in the crystal lattice of diamond are common.
The defects affect the material properties of diamond and determine to which type a diamond is assigned.
5: Condensed matter physics is a branch of physics that deals with the physical properties of condensed phases of matter.
The most familiar condensed phases are solids and liquids.
8: Liquid crystals are matter in a state which has properties between those of conventional liquids and those of solid crystals.
There are many different types of liquid-crystal phases, which can be distinguished by their different optical properties.

topic.name: Jainism and Its Principles
topic.description: The documents explore the core principles and beliefs of Jainism, including ahimsa, karma, and the philosophy surrounding non-violence and moral conduct.

1: Jainism is an ancient Indian religion belonging to the śramaṇa tradition.
The three main principles of Jainism are ahimsa, anekantavada, aparigraha.
2: Karma is the basic principle within an overarching psycho-cosmology in Jainism.
Human moral actions form the basis of the transmigration of the soul.
4: Ahimsā in Jainism is a fundamental principle forming the cornerstone of its ethics and doctrine.
The term ahimsa means nonviolence, non-injury or absence of desire to harm any life forms.

topic.description: The documents discuss various rivers in North America, highlighting their geographical features, historical significance, and ecological importance.

8: Darby Creek is a tributary of the Delaware River in Pennsylvania.
The watershed of the creek is largely developed, with roughly half a million people inhabiting it.
9: Mount Adams is a potentially active stratovolcano in the Cascade Range.
Adams is a member of the Cascade Volcanic Arc.

More than 11,000 years ago, prehistoric Native Americans lived along the Snake.

topic.name: Meteorological Phenomena
topic.description: The documents discuss various atmospheric and oceanic phenomena, including cyclones, wind shear, and the formation of low-pressure areas.

1: Abyssal plain is an underwater plain on the deep ocean floor
active oceanic basins also typically include an oceanic trench and a subduction zone.
2: Wind shear is a difference in wind speed and/or direction over a relatively short distance in the atmosphere
Wind shear has a significant effect during take-off and landing of aircraft.
3: The main effects of tropical cyclones include heavy rain, strong wind, large storm surges at landfall, and tornadoes
Nearly two million people have died globally due to tropical cyclones.
4: Jet streams are fast flowing, narrow, meandering air currents found in the upper atmosphere
The strongest jet streams are the polar jets.
5: A low-pressure area is a region where the atmospheric pressure is lower than that of surrounding locations
The formation process of a low-pressure area is known as cyclogenesis.
6: A mesoscale convective system is a complex of thunderstorms that becomes organized
MCS that develop within the tropics use either the Intertropical Convergence Zone or monsoon troughs.
7: Tropical cyclogenesis is the development and strengthening of a tropical cyclone in the atmosphere
There are six main requirements for tropical cyclogenesis.
8: A desert is a barren area of land where little precipitation occurs
Deserts can be classified by the amount of precipitation that falls.
9: A thunderstorm is a storm characterized by the presence of lightning and its acoustic effect on the Earth's atmosphere
Thunderstorms result from the rapid upward movement of warm, moist air.
10: A cyclone is a large scale air mass that rotates around a strong center of low atmospheric pressure
Cyclogenesis describes the process of cyclone formation and intensification.

topic.name: Evolution of Rock Music
topic.description: This topic encompasses the development and diversification of various rock music genres from the 1960s to the 2000s, highlighting their roots, influences, and cultural significance.

1: Folk metal is a fusion genre of heavy metal music and traditional folk music that developed in Europe during the 1990s.

2: Psychedelic rock is a style of rock music that is inspired or influenced by psychedelic culture.
It emerged as a genre during the mid-1960s among folk rock and blues rock bands in the United Kingdom and United States.
3: Alternative rock emerged from the independent music underground of the 1980s and became widely popular in the 1990s and 2000s.
The term 'alternative' refers to the genre's distinction from mainstream rock music.
4: Graduation marked a movement away from the ornate, soul-based sound of West's previous releases toward a more anthemic style.
Inspired by indie rock and Chicago house, it incorporated synthesizer sounds into his production.
5: British rhythm and blues was a musical movement that developed in the United Kingdom between the late 1950s and the early 1960s.
Major acts included the Rolling Stones, Manfred Mann, and The Animals.
6: Garage rock is a raw and energetic style of rock and roll that flourished in the mid-1960s.
The style, a precursor to psychedelic rock, is often characterized by aggressive and unsophisticated lyrics.
8: Christian metal is a form of heavy metal music usually defined by its message using song lyrics as well as the dedication of the band members to Christianity.
It emerged in the late 1970s as a means of evangelization to the wider heavy metal music scene.
9: Hard rock is a loosely defined subgenre of rock music that began in the mid-1960s.
It developed into a major form of popular music in the 1970s, with bands such as Led Zeppelin and AC/DC.
10: Progressive rock originated in the United Kingdom and United States throughout the mid-to-late 1960s.
It developed from psychedelic rock, and began as an attempt to give greater artistic weight and credibility to rock music.

1: Ontario Highway 400 is a 400-series highway in Ontario linking Toronto with northern regions.
It serves as the primary route from Toronto to southern Georgian Bay and Muskoka.
2: The A1 in London is the southern part of the A1 road, connecting London to Edinburgh.
It was designated in 1921 under the Great Britain road numbering scheme.
3: The Homestead Extension of Florida's Turnpike is a toll road connecting Florida City to the Turnpike mainline.
It is used by commuters and travelers to the Florida Keys and Everglades National Park.
4: Ontario Highway 427 connects the QEW and Gardiner Expressway with York Regional Road 7.
It is Ontario's second busiest freeway by volume.
5: The A4232 is a distributor road in Cardiff, forming part of the Cardiff ring road system.
Construction of the PDR began in 1978 and was completed in sections until 1995.
6: Highway 407 is a tolled highway in Ontario, known for being the first electronically-operated toll highway in the world.
It travels across the Greater Toronto Area (GTA) and has significant interchanges with other major highways.
7: The Arroyo Seco Parkway is the first freeway in the Western United States, connecting Los Angeles with Pasadena.
It is notable for representing the transitional phase between early parkways and modern freeways.
8: The A3 motorway in Croatia connects Zagreb to the Slavonia region, representing a major east-west transportation corridor.
It is part of the Pan-European Corridor X.
9: The A9 motorway in Croatia runs from Pula to the Croatian motorway network, significantly impacting tourism.
Its construction began in 1988 and was completed in 2006.
10: Mitchell Freeway in Perth links central Perth with Joondalup, with planning beginning in the 1950s.
It has been progressively extended since the 1970s.

topic.name: The Boat Race History
topic.description: A historical overview of the annual rowing competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge, detailing significant races and outcomes.

Document 1: The 66th Boat Race took place on 3 April 1909.
Oxford won by three-and-a-half lengths in a time of 19 minutes 50 seconds.
Document 2: The 91st Boat Race took place on 1 April 1939.
Cambridge won by four lengths in a time of 19 minutes 3 seconds.
Document 3: The 121st Boat Race took place on 29 March 1975.
Cambridge won their seventh race in eight years by three-and-three-quarter lengths.
Document 4: The 90th Boat Race took place on 2 April 1938.
Oxford won by two lengths in a time of 20 minutes 30 seconds.
Document 5: The 116th Boat Race took place on 28 March 1970.
It was won by Cambridge who passed the finishing post three-and-a-half lengths ahead of Oxford.
Document 6: The 86th Boat Race took place on 17 March 1934.
Cambridge won by four-and-a-quarter lengths in a time of 18 minutes 3 seconds.
Document 7: The 89th Boat Race took place on 24 March 1937.
Oxford won by three lengths in a time of 22 minutes 39 seconds.
Document 8: The 112th Boat Race took place on 26 March 1966.
The race was won by Oxford by three-and-three-quarter-lengths.
Document 9: The 96th Boat Race took place on 1 April 1950.
Cambridge won by three-and-a-half lengths in a time of 20 minutes 15 seconds.
Document 10: The 73rd Boat Race took place on 30 March 1921.
Cambridge won by one length in a time of 19 minutes 45 seconds.

topic.description: This cluster discusses various professional baseball players who have participated in Major League Baseball (MLB), highlighting their careers, achievements, and backgrounds.

Document 1: Joe Kelley was an American left fielder in Major League Baseball (MLB) who starred in the outfield of the Baltimore Orioles teams of the 1890s.
Kelley received the nickname 'Kingpin of the Orioles'.
Document 2: Curtis Granderson is an American professional baseball outfielder for the New York Mets of Major League Baseball (MLB).
Granderson is a three-time MLB All-Star and won the Silver Slugger Award in 2011.
Document 3: Joey Hamilton is a former Major League Baseball pitcher who played professionally for nine seasons.
Hamilton started his professional career in 1991 when he was drafted by the San Diego Padres.
Document 4: Tyler Clippard is an American professional baseball pitcher for the Arizona Diamondbacks of Major League Baseball (MLB).
Clippard was named to the MLB All-Star Game in 2011 and 2014.
Document 5: John Samuel 'Jocko' Thompson was a professional baseball pitcher who played all or part of four seasons for the Philadelphia Phillies.
Thompson played in Major League Baseball during the Whiz Kids era.
Document 6: Jonathan Brian Weber is a professional baseball player who has played professionally in minor league baseball since 1999.
Weber has competed for the United States national baseball team, winning the gold medal at the 2009 Baseball World Cup.
Document 7: Alfred Stanley 'Stan' Benjamin was a right fielder in Major League Baseball for five seasons.
Benjamin played in minor league baseball for ten seasons.
Document 8: Daniel Thomas Meyer is a retired professional baseball player whose career spanned 15 seasons, 12 of which were played in Major League Baseball (MLB).
Meyer made his major league debut in 1974.
Document 9: Patrick Alan Corbin is an American professional baseball pitcher for the Arizona Diamondbacks of Major League Baseball (MLB).
Corbin was named an All-Star in 2013.
Document 10: Douglas Wildes Fister is an American professional baseball pitcher for the Houston Astros of Major League Baseball (MLB).
Fister spent four seasons in the Seattle Mariners minor league organization before being promoted to their Major League roster.

topic.name: Military Units and Commands
topic.description: The documents discuss various military units and commands from the United States and Australia, focusing on their histories, roles, and deployments during and after World War II.

Document 1: 45th Infantry Brigade Combat Team is a modular infantry brigade combat team of the United States Army.
The brigade deployed as part of the UN peacekeeping force in the wake of the Bosnian War.
Document 2: Western Area Command was one of several geographically based commands raised by the Royal Australian Air Force during World War II.
It was primarily responsible for air defence, aerial reconnaissance and protection of the sea lanes.
Document 3: North-Eastern Area Command was one of several geographically based commands raised by the Royal Australian Air Force during World War II.

Document 4: The 42nd Military Police Brigade is a military police brigade of the United States Army based at Joint Base Lewis-McChord.

Document 6: No. 5 Airfield Construction Squadron was a Royal Australian Air Force construction squadron.

Document 7: The 20th Engineer Brigade is a combat engineer brigade assigned to the XVIII Airborne Corps of the United States Army.

Document 10: The 172nd Infantry Brigade was an infantry brigade of the United States Army headquartered at Grafenwoehr, Germany.

topic.name: 1948 Australian Cricket Team
topic.description: The documents discuss the achievements and key players of the Australian cricket team that toured England in 1948, famously known as 'The Invincibles'.

1: Bill Johnston was a key member of Donald Bradman 's famous Australian cricket team , which toured England in 1948.
the Australians went undefeated in their 34 matches during the English summer ; this unprecedented feat earned them the sobriquet The Invincibles.
2: Ray Lindwall MBE ( 3 October 1921 – 23 June 1996 ) was a cricketer who represented Australia in 61 Tests from 1946 to 1960.
Lindwall formed a new-ball pairing regarded as one of the greatest to have played cricket.
3: Ian Johnson was a member of Donald Bradman 's famous Australian cricket team , which toured England in 1948.
Bradman ’ s men went undefeated in their 34 matches ; this unprecedented feat earned them the sobriquet The Invincibles.
4: Arthur Lindsay Hassett MBE was a cricketer who played for Victoria and Australia.
Hassett was a crucial role in Australia 's win in the Fourth Test, with a composed display in the run-chase which sealed the retention of the Ashes.
5: Keith Miller , AM MBE was an Australian test cricketer and a Royal Australian Air Force pilot during World War II.
A member of the record-breaking Invincibles, at the time of his retirement from Test cricket in 1956, Miller had the best statistics of any all-rounder in cricket history.
6: Arthur Morris was a key member of Donald Bradman 's famous Australian cricket team , which toured England in 1948.
Morris ended the first-class tour with 1,922 runs at a batting average of 71.18 including seven centuries.
8: The 1948 Ashes series was that year 's edition of the long-standing cricket rivalry between England and Australia.
Australia won 10 of their 12 lead-up matches, eight by an innings.
9: Neil Harvey is a former Australian cricketer who represented the Australian cricket team between 1948 and 1963.
Harvey was the youngest member of the 1948 Invincibles of Don Bradman to tour England.
10: The Australian cricket team in England in 1948 was captained by Don Bradman.
This feat earned them the nickname of The Invincibles, and they are regarded as one of the greatest cricket teams of all time.

topic.name: Military History of New Zealand and Australia
topic.description: The documents discuss the formation, service, and historical significance of various military battalions from New Zealand and Australia during World War I and World War II.

Document 1: The 28th ( Māori ) Battalion was an infantry battalion of the New Zealand Army that served during the Second World War.
It was formed following pressure on the Labour government by some Māori Members of Parliament ( MPs ) and Māori organisations throughout the country.
Document 2: The 15th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army.
Formed in 1914 as part of the all-volunteer Australian Imperial Force from Queensland and Tasmanian recruits.
Document 3: The 2nd Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army.
It was initially raised for service during the First World War as part the Australian Imperial Force.
Document 4: The Canterbury Mounted Rifles Regiment was a mounted infantry regiment from New Zealand, raised for service during the First World War.
It was assigned to the New Zealand Mounted Rifles Brigade, and formed part of the New Zealand Expeditionary Force.
Document 5: The Wellington Mounted Rifles Regiment was a mounted infantry regiment from New Zealand, raised for service during the First World War.
It was assigned to the New Zealand Mounted Rifles Brigade, part of the New Zealand Expeditionary Force.
Document 6: In Australia, the outbreak of World War I was greeted with considerable enthusiasm.
The first campaign that Australians were involved in was in German New Guinea.
